sensitivity: impacted third molars: 99%, periapical radiolucency: 82%, caries: 77%
auc: impacted third molars (wAFROC lesion): 0.9788, periapical radiolucency (wAFROC lesion): 0.8113, caries (wAFROC lesion): 0.7211
intended_use: Second Opinion® Panoramic is a radiological automated image processing software device intended to identify and mark regions, in panoramic radiographs, in relation to suspected dental findings which i
intended_use_full: Second Opinion® Panoramic is a radiological automated image processing software device intended to identify and mark regions, in panoramic radiographs, in relation to suspected dental findings which include: Caries, Periapical radiolucency, and Impacted third molars. It is designed to aid dental health professionals to review panoramic radiographs of permanent teeth in patients 16 years of age or older as both a concurrent and second reader.
target_condition: caries, periapical radiolucency, and impacted third molars in panoramic radiographs
ground_truth: consensus approach based on agreement among at least three out of four expert board-certified dentists with a minimum of five years practice experience, who independently marked caries and periapical radiolucencies using polygons and impactions using bounding boxes
other_metrics: wAFROC image level: impacted third molars 0.99, periapical radiolucency 0.91, caries 0.90; Dice coefficient ≥0.68; Jaccard index ≥0.62; MRMC study lesion-level wAFROC differences: periapical radiolucency 0.0705 (p<0.00001), caries 0.0306 (p=0.0195), impacted teeth 0.0093 (p=0.0326); MRMC sensitivity gains: periapical radiolucency 0.2045, caries 0.1169, impacted teeth 0.0192
